steps.n: These are special letters that appear at the start of some chapters of the Quran. They are read by pronouncing each letter's name: 'Ha' then 'Meem'.

steps.m: Ha Meem (special letters at chapter start)
steps.n: The long mark (ٓ) shows these letters are special chapter openers, not regular words.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The extra ي and pattern change makes it mean doing something gradually or repeatedly
The added 'u' sound at the end shows this word is the subject or doer in the sentence. It owns or possesses the next word الكتاب (the book)
Changes from a verb (to write) into a noun (a book/written record)
Adds ٱلْ to make it specific - this isn't just any book, but a particular book being referred to

Adds ِ (kasra) at the end to show this book is being possessed by something (in this case, it's connected to تَنزِيلُ meaning 'revelation'). Also changes the alif to a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing variation.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word (اللَّهِ). This shows that 'from' and 'Allah' are linked together in meaning.
Added emphasis (shadda) to show the strength and uniqueness of Allah

steps.m: of Allah (owner of ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ - the Mighty, the Knowing)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif at start. This shows Allah owns or possesses the attributes mentioned next (the Mighty, the Knowing).
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to show this is a permanent, intense quality

steps.m: The Mighty, The Powerful (Divine attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes this a definite Divine attribute of Allah in this context
The kasra ending shows this is describing Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah
The ي between the second and third root letters shows this is a strong, permanent quality. When used for Allah, it means His knowledge is perfect and complete.
Adding ال makes this a specific title rather than a general description. Here it's one of Allah's divine names.
The kasra at the end shows this is describing Allah's name which came just before it in the ayah (ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ). It's one of several attributes listed together.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter and kasra after second root letter to create the active participle pattern showing who does the action
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or possesses what comes after it (الذَّنۢبِ meaning 'sin')

steps.m: to commit a wrong or make a mistake

steps.n: Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'sin/mistake' by adding vowel marks
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter. This word is what is being forgiven in غَافِرِ (Forgiver of)

steps.m: of the wrong action (object of غَافِرِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to or connects with the word before it (غَافِرِ - Forgiver of)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added kasra at the end to show this word owns or is connected to the next word (التوب - repentance)
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word with the previous word (غافر - forgiver)

steps.m: to return from wrong to right

steps.n: Changes verb 'to repent' into noun meaning 'one instance of repentance'
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the repentance' instead of just 'repentance'
Added kasra at the end to show this word describes 'forgiver' in the phrase 'forgiver of repentance'
Added vowels and ي to make an adjective showing strong quality. This word describes العِقَابِ (punishment) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the next word (العِقَابِ), like saying 'of strong' or 'strong's'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun by adding alif and changing first vowel to kasra
Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by شَدِيدِ (severe/intense), forming the phrase 'severe in punishment'
This is a special word that shows ownership or possession. It connects two words together like 'of' in English.
Added kasra to connect it with what comes after it
Added ى at the end to complete the possessive meaning. This word connects to ٱلطَّوْلِ (bounty/favor) to say 'possessor of bounty'.

steps.m: power/might (from the idea of extending far)
steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to create a noun meaning power (something that extends far)
Added ال to make it specific and shadda because of sun letter ط

steps.m: of the power/might (possessed by ذِى - owner of)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this describes what ذِى (owner of) possesses. Also changed alif to wasl form since it's standard in Quran writing.
Removes the kasra (i) ending that shows possession
This is a negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. It negates the word after it (إِلٰهَ meaning 'god'), creating the meaning 'there is no god'.
Added fatha mark on the first letter to show how it's pronounced
Changed the alif to a long alif (آ) to show it's pronounced with a longer sound

steps.m: to worship or take as a deity

steps.n: Root letters أله became the noun إِلَه meaning 'god' by adding the vowel marks
Added fatha at the end because it comes after لا (meaning 'no/there is not'). The alif is written as a small mark (ٰ) above the letter ه in this word.
This is a basic particle that shows something is being excluded. It's like saying 'but not' or 'except for' in English.
Added kasra under إ to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: except (excluding what comes after it from هُوَ)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it's excluding what comes after it (هُوَ) from what came before.

This is a basic pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah who is described in the previous phrases (the Forgiver of sin, Accepter of repentance, etc). This pronoun is the subject of the phrase إِلَيْهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (to Him is the destination).
This is a preposition meaning 'to' or 'toward'
The ى changes to ي with sukun before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change
Added هِ which means 'him' at the end to show who the action is directed to

steps.m: to move or change from one state to another
place or state something ends up in
steps.n: Adding مَ at the beginning turns this into a noun showing where or how something ends up

steps.m: the final destination (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific (like 'the' in English), and ُ shows it's the subject
The word means 'destination' and has a -u ending showing it's the main subject
This is a special word that makes the verb after it negative. In this ayah, it changes 'argues/disputes' to 'does not argue/dispute'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding ا shows two people are arguing with each other, not just one person arguing

steps.m: he argues/disputes (with ءَايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ - the signs of Allah)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. The ُ at the end shows this is happening now.
This is a basic preposition that shows something happens inside or within something else. Here it connects the verb يُجٰدِلُ (argue/dispute) with what is being argued about (ءَايٰتِ - signs/verses).
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing rule and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb to a noun meaning 'sign/miracle'. Two letters (أ and ي) merged into one long letter (آ)
Changed to plural form to show multiple signs/miracles
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ). The alif was written as a long alif (ٰ) which is just a writing style.
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to or describes something else. Here it shows these are 'the signs of Allah'.

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a fundamental particle used for exception/exclusion. It doesn't have a root-based derivation.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to people or things
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا۟)

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb. This word describes the people mentioned in الَّذِينَ who argue about Allah's signs.
Added وا with ُ before it to show many people did this action. The small mark (۟) at the end is just to help with pronunciation.
This is the basic negation word that stops something from happening
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result of what came before. In this ayah, it's saying that because of what was mentioned before, something should not deceive you.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
The كَ at the end shows who is being tricked (you). This word appears in the phrase 'let not deceive you' (فَلَا يَغْرُرْكَ) where فَلَا means 'so do not'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled ل to show repeated self-action
Changed vowels to make it a noun showing the action itself
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Added هُمْ to show it belongs to them, connecting to فِى ٱلْبِلٰدِ (in the lands) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that the arguing/disputing happens within or about Allah's signs/verses.
Added ى at the end to complete the word. This is a spelling convention for this preposition.

steps.m: to live in a place, settle down

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a noun meaning a place
Changes to broken plural form to mean multiple countries/lands
Added ال to make it specific lands being referred to
The word changed to show it comes after 'in' (فِى) - like saying 'in the lands'. The long 'aa' sound is written slightly differently too.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb

steps.m: to strongly deny or reject something as false
steps.n: Doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger - from simple 'denied' to 'strongly rejected as false'
Adding ت with sukun at the end shows the subject is feminine (she). This verb comes before قَبْلَهُمْ (before them) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be in front of, to precede

steps.n: Forms a noun meaning 'before' from the root letters

steps.m: before them (referring to the people who denied the truth)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, 'them' refers to the people who denied the truth.

steps.m: to stand upright, rise up, establish

steps.n: The root meaning 'to stand' becomes a noun for 'people' - like a group standing together as one
The damma (ُ) shows this word is the subject doing the action of denying/rejecting in the verse
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a root. It refers to the prophet Noah who was sent to his people before they were destroyed by the flood.
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show standard pronunciation of the name
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is possessed by the word before it (قَوْمُ meaning 'people'). Together they mean 'people of Noah'.

steps.m: to join together in a group

steps.n: Root letters combined to make a noun meaning 'group' - like pieces joining together
Makes the word plural to show multiple groups
Adds 'the' to specify these particular groups and adds ُ to show this is the subject doing something
Adds 'and' to connect this with what came before
This is a basic preposition that connects two time periods in this ayah - showing movement from 'before them' to 'after them'. It helps show sequence in time.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a usable word
The word gets a kasra (-i) sound at the end because it connects to the next word

steps.m: after them (referring to the previous nations mentioned in قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَٱلْأَحْزَابُ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show it means 'after them', referring back to Noah's people and the confederates mentioned earlier in the verse
The root conveys the idea of planning or having a purpose
Connected the root letters with fatha and added shadda to show strong intention. The shadda doubles the middle letter to intensify the meaning.
The تْ at the end changes 'he' to 'she'. In this ayah, this refers to each nation (أُمَّةٍ) trying to seize their messenger.

steps.m: and she intended strongly (subject of لِيَأْخُذُوهُ)
steps.n: The وَ (and) connects this to the previous phrase about the nations that came after the people of Noah, showing a sequence of events
This is a special word that shows totality or completeness
Adding the damma and doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'every' or 'all'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject doing something in the sentence
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create the base noun form
Adding ة changes it from 'mother/source' to mean a whole community
Added two dots under the word to show it's 'a nation' rather than 'the nation'
The root letters represent the basic concept of sending or delivering something
The و after س changes it from 'sending' to 'one who sends/delivers frequently'
Adds بِ at the beginning to show how the messenger is involved in the action, and adds ِ at the end because it follows بِ
Adds هِمْ at the end to show the messenger belongs to them
The basic meaning of taking or grabbing something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و to show multiple people doing the action
Added لِ to show purpose or intention

steps.m: so that they may seize him (object of هَمَّتْ/intended)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who is being seized. This connects to the word هَمَّتْ (intended) in the verse.
The root conveys the idea of arguing or debating
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he argued back and forth with someone
steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show this is an argument between two parties

steps.m: they argued back and forth with someone
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action

steps.m: and they argued back and forth with someone
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before it
The root relates to falsehood or invalidity
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changed middle vowel to kasra to show something becoming false
Changed from a verb (became false) to a noun (false thing) and added 'the' at the start

steps.m: with the false thing (used to argue against الْحَقَّ)
steps.n: Added preposition بِ meaning 'with'. In this ayah, it shows how they used falsehood to argue against the truth (الْحَقَّ).
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: he caused something to slip away
steps.n: Added hamza at beginning to show causing something to happen

steps.m: he causes something to slip away
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they cause something to slip away
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: so that they may cause (the truth) to slip away
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose or intention. In this ayah, they are trying to make the truth disappear through arguing.
Added kasra to the preposition ب to show it means 'with'

steps.m: with it (referring to الْحَقَّ - the truth)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (it/him) to show what is being done with. In this ayah, it refers back to الْحَقَّ (the truth) that they were trying to reject.
The root relates to truth and rightness
Adding the fatha vowel mark makes it a past tense verb
Root حَقق became a noun meaning 'truth', then ال was added to make it 'THE truth'

steps.m: the truth (which they try to reject)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon - it's the truth that they are trying to reject
The basic meaning of taking or seizing
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Added تُ to show 'I' did the action, and changed the middle vowel to sukun
Added هُمْ to show who was taken (them)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something before it
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding these vowel marks creates the standard question word 'how'
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question to the previous statement about Allah punishing people. It shows this question follows from what was just mentioned.
The root means to exist or occur
Added short vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of verb with a weak middle letter. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
The root relates to following or consequence

Added ِ (kasra) on first letter and ا (alif) after second letter to make it mean the result of following - in this case, punishment. This word is the subject of كَانَ (was) in the question 'how was My punishment?'
The kasra ending here shows possession - 'My punishment' - as this is referring to Allah's punishment in the ayah
Basic demonstrative particle meaning 'this' or 'that'
Added لِ to specify distance (making it mean 'that' rather than 'this')
Added كَ (ka) at start to mean 'like/similar to' and at end to mean 'that way' - making it mean 'like that'
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to the following statement حَقَّتْ (became due/established)

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis and fatha to show past tense. This word describes what happened to كَلِمَتُ (the word/decree) in this ayah.

steps.m: she/it became proven true (referring to كَلِمَتُ)
steps.n: Added ت to match with كَلِمَتُ (the word/decree) which is feminine in Arabic
Adding vowels to make it a basic past tense verb meaning 'to speak'
Changed from 'he spoke' (verb) to 'a word' (noun) by adding ة at the end
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb حَقَّتْ (came true/was proven true)

steps.m: to care for and help grow
steps.n: Forms the intensive noun 'lord' with shadda for emphasis

steps.m: lord (one who cares for and helps others grow)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the second letter with shadda to show intensity, making it mean 'the one who does this action a lot'

steps.m: lord (one who cares for and helps others grow)
steps.n: Added kasra under the doubled letter to prepare for adding the possession ending
Added كَ at the end to show it means 'your Lord'
This is a preposition that shows something being on top of or over something else. In this ayah, it connects 'the word of your Lord' to 'those who disbelieved', showing that the word came upon them.
Added kasra and ي to make the basic relative pronoun
Added نَ to make it plural
Added ٱلْ at the beginning and shadda on ذ to make it definite. This word is the subject of كَفَرُوٓا۟ (they disbelieved) in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject or deny truth by covering it up

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Added elongation mark (ـٓ) and small high rounded zero (۟) as pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning
This is a particle that makes what comes after it more certain and emphasized, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: that they (emphasizes they are أَصْحٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ (they/them) to show who is being talked about. This connects to the next phrase أَصْحٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ (companions of the Fire), showing that they are the companions of the Fire.

steps.m: to accompany or be a close friend

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make it mean 'he accompanied' in the past
Changed to describe someone who does the action
Changes from single companion (صَاحِب) to multiple companions by using a common plural pattern
Changed alif to alif maqsurah for writing style, no meaning change

steps.m: companions (as the doers/subjects of the sentence)
steps.n: Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The middle letter و changes to ا to form the noun 'fire' - a special type of light
ال makes it 'the fire' instead of just 'fire', and the shadda makes it stronger/more intense
The kasra at the end shows this word follows أَصْحٰبُ (companions) in the ayah
Added ن at the end to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the beginning with a shadda on the ذ to make it specific. This word refers to the angels who carry the throne, as they are the subject of يَحْمِلُونَ (they carry).
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense with يَ prefix and vowel pattern change
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those who carry Allah's throne (العَرْشَ) mentioned in this ayah.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the thing that is raised up - a throne
Adding ال makes it specific - THE throne, not just any throne
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being carried. The word يَحْمِلُونَ means 'they carry' and this throne is what they are carrying.
This is a basic question word that asks about people (who/whoever). In this ayah, it refers to those around Allah's throne.
Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the ayah

steps.m: to go around or surround something

steps.n: Changes verb into a noun meaning 'around'
Added fatha at the end to show this word describes location

steps.m: around him (referring to العرش - the throne)
steps.n: Added pronoun 'him' to show who/what is being surrounded. This refers back to 'the throne' mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to glorify, praise, or swim freely

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with extra effort or repeatedly
Adding يُ at the start and changing vowels shows this happens in the present/future

steps.m: they glorify intensively (referring to those who carry the throne)
steps.n: Adding ون shows multiple people are doing this action. In this ayah, it refers to those who carry Allah's throne.
Changed the concept into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the actual praise itself rather than the act of praising.
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with/by' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word after it (رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord). Together they mean 'with the praise of their Lord'.

steps.m: to take care of, raise, nurture
master who takes complete care (of something/someone)
steps.n: Root 'r-b-b' changed to 'rabb' meaning 'lord/master', with kasra (ِ) showing it owns or connects to what comes next

steps.m: their Lord (the one being glorified with بِحَمْدِ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this is their Lord. This word is being glorified in the phrase بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ (with praise of their Lord)

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, or believe in

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. The hamza gets a sukun (ؤْ) for pronunciation.
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they believe/trust (in what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this action to the previous phrase
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or connected to something else

steps.m: in/with/by him (referring to Allah, acted on by يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Adding هِۦ shows this refers to Allah. This whole phrase connects to the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) to show what they believe in.

steps.m: to forgive or cover up faults

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added سْتَ to show seeking or requesting the action
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: and they seek forgiveness (connected to يُسَبِّحُونَ - they glorify)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this action with the previous verb يُسَبِّحُونَ (they glorify)
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ال and ي changes it from pointing at something to describing who does something
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one

steps.m: for those who (who are being forgiven in فَٱغْفِرْ)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows these people are receiving the action of forgiveness. When لِ is added before ال, the ل gets a shadda (double mark)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from basic trust to religious faith
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together
Adding the double letter (shadda) changes this from a verb (to care for) into a noun meaning 'lord' or 'master' who takes complete care of others
The suffix نا means 'our' and shows we are speaking directly to Allah in prayer. This word appears in a prayer by the angels who carry Allah's throne.
Added vowels to make a past tense verb showing a quality or state

steps.m: you became vast/encompassing (describing Allah's mercy and knowledge)
steps.n: Changed تَ at the end shows this is talking about 'you' instead of 'he'. In this ayah, the angels are speaking to Allah about His vast mercy and knowledge.
The doubled ل with ُ on the first letter turns the idea of 'covering completely' into a word meaning 'every' or 'all'

steps.m: every, all (describing وَسِعْتَ - You have encompassed)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb وَسِعْتَ (You have encompassed)
Root letters ش-ي-أ got vowels (َ ْ) to make the common noun 'thing'
Changed the spelling of ي to ى which is common in Quranic writing but doesn't change the meaning
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word belongs to كُلَّ (every/all) which came before it
Changed from a root meaning 'to show mercy' into a simple noun meaning 'mercy' by adding vowels
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of mercy
Added ً (tanween) to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence
Added shadda on first letter due to connection with previous word
Changed the verb 'to know' into the noun 'knowledge' by adding specific vowel marks. Like changing 'know' to 'knowledge' in English.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with رَحْمَةً (mercy) that came before it. Together they show what Allah encompasses everything with.

steps.m: and knowledge (object of وَسِعْتَ - You encompassed)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this knowledge is being acted upon by the verb وَسِعْتَ (You encompassed). Like saying 'You encompassed everything with knowledge'.

steps.m: to forgive or cover up faults completely

steps.n: Changed to a command by adding 'u' at start and putting special marks on the letters to say 'forgive!'
Changed the vowel mark on first letter from 'u' to 'i' sound

steps.m: so forgive! (command in response to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ (so/then) to connect this command with the previous statement, showing it's a response or result
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding لِ shows this is being done for or to these people. In this ayah, they are being prayed for by the angels carrying Allah's throne.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

هِم is 3rd person masculine plural object pronoun

Adds sukun to mark the end of the word and show it's a command
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Lord (one who takes care of everything)

Added tanween kasra to show this word describes the gardens (جَنَّاتِ) that come before it. In Arabic, this construction means 'Gardens of Eden'.

Changed from 3rd person to 2nd person masculine singular by adding تَ suffix
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was promised
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'whoever' or 'who'. In this ayah, it refers to the people who are righteous/good (صَلَحَ).
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This connects to the previous request about entering Paradise (جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ), adding more people who should enter.
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects to the word ءَابَآئِهِمْ (their fathers) to show they came from their fathers.
Changed from root letters to the word 'father' by adding vowels
Changed from singular 'father' to irregular plural 'fathers' by adding letters
Added kasra to show possession
Added pronoun suffix meaning 'their'

steps.m: to join things together in pairs

steps.n: Adding أَ at the start makes this a broken plural form
Adds هِمْ at the end to show these spouses belong to them (referring back to the believers mentioned earlier in the verse)

Adding -aat at the end makes it mean 'multiple offspring/descendants'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word is being acted upon by something
Adding possessive pronoun suffix meaning 'their'

steps.m: you (masculine singular, being described by الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ)

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show a constant, intense quality rather than an action
Added ال to make it definite and ُ to show it's a subject. This is one of Allah's names, showing His perfect might and power.

steps.m: to judge wisely, make wise decisions

steps.n: Added ي between the last two root letters and vowels to show someone who has the quality of wisdom
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it describes Allah. This is one of Allah's names showing His perfect wisdom.
Command form made by dropping letters and adding kasra (protect!)
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Added هِم at end to show who is being protected
Added vowels to make the basic noun form
Changed from 'bad' to 'very bad' by doubling the middle letter (making it stronger)
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple bad deeds

steps.m: the bad deeds (object of protect from)

This is a relative pronoun meaning 'whoever' or 'who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who protects others from evil.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This shows this protection is an additional good deed being mentioned.

steps.m: to protect or shield from harm

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ِ

Basic root meaning

Basic root meaning
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being described by what comes after it

Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. قَدْ is used to emphasize that something definitely happened or will happen.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this idea with what came before. It shows that one thing leads to another, like saying 'and so' or 'then' in English.

steps.n: Basic root meaning
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Adding تَ at the end changes it from 'he showed mercy' to 'you showed mercy'
Added هُۥ (him) at the end to show who received the mercy
Starting with the basic demonstrative word. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an alif khanjariya
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ completes the demonstrative word. This is a common ending for demonstrative words

steps.m: and that (refers to the reward mentioned in previous ayah)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it, showing this is talking about the reward mentioned in the previous ayah
Basic independent pronoun that refers back to ذٰلِكَ (that) in this ayah. It connects what was mentioned before (being protected from bad deeds) with what comes after (the great success).
Basic root meaning
Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks - fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific, like saying 'the' in English

steps.m: the success (subject of هُوَ - it is)
steps.n: Added damma at the end because this word is the subject after هُوَ (it is) in this ayah
Basic root meaning
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong, permanent quality. This word is describing Allah in this ayah.
Added 'The' (ال) at the start to make it specific, and a small mark (ُ) at the end to show it's the main word
Added kasra under إ to show correct pronunciation
Added shadda to strengthen the ن sound. This makes the word emphasize what comes after it more strongly. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about those who disbelieved.
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ to make it specific. This word points to one specific person or thing being described.
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word refers to multiple people and connects to كَفَرُوا۟ (they disbelieved) in this ayah.

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense. This word describes those who are being called out in يُنَادَوْنَ (they are called).
Adding وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they (masculine plural)'. These are the same people who are being called out in يُنَادَوْنَ.
The ۟ mark (small circle) shows this is a plural form
Added alif to show interaction between two parties (like caller and called)

steps.m: he is being called out to
steps.n: Changed from active (he called) to passive (being called to) by adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels

steps.m: they are being called out to

Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely true
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific deity
Shows this word is being described by another word
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: than (comparing Allah's hatred to their self-hatred)

steps.m: to hate strongly or detest completely

Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.
Added يُ prefix and changed vowels to show passive voice (something being done to someone)
Changed يُ to تُ to show 'you' instead of 'he'
Added وْنَ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed
This is a preposition that shows direction. In this ayah, it shows they are being called towards faith (ٱلْإِيمٰنِ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to create a noun meaning 'the act or state of believing'

steps.m: when (followed by دُعِىَ - was called upon)

steps.n: Added the pronoun هُۥ (him) to show who is alone. In this ayah, it refers to Allah being called upon alone without partners.

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth

steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about disbelief. It shows this is another related situation being described.

steps.m: something is joined (as a partner with Allah)

Added the preposition بِ (with) to the pronoun هِ (him/it). This shows that something is being done with or by means of the thing being referred to.

تُ shows it's either 'you' (male) or 'she' doing the action

Changed the word pattern to show a strong quality. The last letter changes to ي with shadda after kasra to show intensity.

Added kasra at the end because this word describes and matches the case of ٱلْكَبِيرِ (The Great) which follows it

steps.m: the Great (One) (describing Allah, connected to العَلِىِّ)

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this ayah)

steps.m: The one who (connects to يُرِيكُمْ - shows you)

Basic past tense form of the verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form

Basic past tense form of the verb

Adding shadda makes the action more intense or repeated
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

Added fatha to the letter ل to show its basic pronunciation. This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose.

steps.m: for you all (receiving provision from السَّمَآءِ)
steps.n: Added كم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This word connects to السَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven) showing that provision comes from there to you all.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of something
The shadda (ّ) shows that when this word connects to the word before it in speech, the last letter of that word combines with the م
The fatha (َ) at the end shows this connects to the next word ٱلسَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven), making the complete phrase 'from the sky/heaven'

Changed from 'rising up' action to the place above us (sky) by adding ء at end
Added ال (the) at the start to make it mean 'the sky' instead of just 'sky'

steps.m: a provision (object of يُنَزِّلُ - He sends down)
steps.n: Added tanween (double line) to show this is the object - the thing being sent down

steps.m: and not (connects to يُرِيكُمْ - shows you)

Adding shadda makes it mean 'to make someone else remember'
Adding يَتَ at the start shows ongoing action and reflexive meaning (doing to oneself). The ُ at the end shows this is happening now.

This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it refers to the people who turn back to Allah, as it's the subject of يُنِيبُ (turns back/repents).
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding fatha marks (َ) and making the middle letter alif
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed middle vowels to match present tense pattern
Changed vowel pattern to show returning in a spiritual sense (repenting). This word is connected to يَتَذَكَّرُ (remembers/takes heed) in the ayah, showing who does the remembering.

steps.m: to call upon, invite, pray to

Adding ين to the end makes it 'multiple people who are sincere' instead of just one person

steps.m: to move upward step by step

steps.n: Root letters connected with 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the plural noun meaning 'steps'
Added ة to show one specific rank or level
Changed to plural form showing multiple ranks
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the د with shadda

steps.m: of the ranks/levels (possessed by رَفِيعُ - The Exalted)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word is possessed by رَفِيعُ (The Exalted One)
This is a special word that doesn't come from a typical root. It's one of the basic words in Arabic.
Added damma (ُ) to show this word is describing رَفِيعُ (The Exalted) in this ayah. Together they mean 'The Exalted One, Owner of...'

steps.m: to raise up, elevate, build high

steps.n: Adding vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'throne'
The ال makes this a specific throne rather than any throne
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and changed middle vowel to sukun
Changed vowels to make it passive - something is being thrown rather than throwing
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he breathed) to a noun (spirit/soul) by changing the middle vowel
Adding ال makes it specific and doubles the ر (shown by ّ)

steps.m: the spirit (object of يُلْقِى - He casts/sends down)
steps.n: The fatha shows this is receiving the action of يُلْقِى (sends down/casts)

steps.m: from (connects أَمْرِهِۦ 'His command' to what comes after)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or is part of something. In this ayah, it connects 'His command' to those it comes to.

steps.m: to command/order, or to make something happen

steps.n: Changes from action to a noun meaning command or matter. This is what Allah sends down.
Adds kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after the word مِنْ (from)
Adds هِۦ (his) to show this is Allah's command that is being sent down

steps.m: upon (referring to يُلْقِى - he casts/sends down)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is being sent down upon or onto something else. In this ayah, it shows that Allah sends down the spirit upon whomever He wills from His servants.

This is a special word that refers to unspecified people - in this ayah it refers to whichever people Allah chooses from among His servants. It connects to the verb يَشَآءُ (He wills) that comes right after it.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added fatha vowels and the middle weak letter ي changes to ا, which is common in Arabic for this type of word
Changed from past tense (he wanted) to present tense (he wants) by adding يَ at the start
Added long alif to show extended/ongoing wanting
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows that Allah casts the spirit from His command.
The root letters show the basic concept of serving or worshipping
Root letters ع-ب-د became a noun عَبْد by adding vowels - changes from 'to serve' into 'a servant'
Changes from singular 'servant' to plural 'servants' using broken plural pattern
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show these are His servants. The ۦ is just a small mark to help with reading. The word comes after مِنْ (min) which means 'from/of' in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding يُ at the start changes this from past tense (he warned) to present tense (he warns). The middle letter also gets a different vowel mark.

steps.m: so that he may warn (purpose of يُلْقِى - he sends down)
steps.n: The لِ shows this is the purpose for sending down the spirit mentioned earlier (يُلْقِى ٱلرُّوحَ). The َ at the end shows this is in the subjunctive mood because it follows لِ.
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic noun for 'day'
The word takes a fatha at the end because it's what's being warned about in لِيُنذِرَ (to warn). It specifically refers to the Day of Meeting mentioned right after this word.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun form that can be possessed by the word يَوْمَ (day), making it mean 'day of meeting'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ت because it comes after ال
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'day'
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case
This is a standalone pronoun that means 'they' or 'them'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word بٰرِزُونَ (appearing/emerging), showing who is appearing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes from a verb (emerged) to describing someone who is emerging. This describes what 'they' (هم) are doing in the ayah.

steps.m: those who are emerging/appearing (subject of هم)
steps.n: Makes the word plural to show it's describing multiple people. The alif changes to a long alif (ٰ). This word describes what 'they' (هم) are doing on that Day.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it makes يَخْفَىٰ (hidden) mean 'not hidden'
Added vowels to make the past tense form
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start. The middle vowel changes and the ي at the end becomes ى because it's a special verb ending in ي
Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. Here it connects with اللَّهِ (Allah) to show that nothing is hidden from (upon) Allah.
Added emphasis (shadda) on the ل because this word is so important
Added kasra at the end to show possession - Allah owns the dominion mentioned in this ayah. Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Basic preposition indicating source or origin

steps.m: from them (referring to those who are exposed/visible in this verse)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُمْ (them) to show who the preposition applies to. This refers back to the people mentioned as بٰرِزُونَ (exposed/visible) in this verse.
The root letters ش-ي-أ form the common noun شَيْء (thing) by adding a fatha on ش and a sukun on ي. This word is used as the subject - telling us what cannot be hidden from Allah.
Adding the tanween (double damma) makes this an indefinite noun - meaning 'a thing' rather than 'the thing'
This is a question word that asks about people. It's like saying 'who' or 'whom' in English.
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it.
Adding لِ at the beginning changes the meaning to 'for whom'. This word connects to الْمُلْكُ (the dominion) in this ayah, asking 'for whom is the dominion?'
The shadda (ّ) on the ل shows emphasis and indicates this word blends with the sound of the word before it.
Changed from a basic verb (to rule) into a noun meaning the power/control itself by adding vowels
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it belongs to Allah in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun

steps.m: a day (object of time when بٰرِزُونَ/emerging happens)
steps.n: Shows when something happens by adding a mark at the end

steps.m: the day (object of time when بٰرِزُونَ/emerging happens)
steps.n: Adds ال (the) to make it 'the day' instead of just 'day'
This is God's proper name in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
Added لِ at the beginning to show that something belongs to Allah. The ِ at the end connects this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it shows that the dominion (ٱلْمُلْكُ) belongs to Allah.
Added ا after و and vowel marks to make it mean 'one' (like counting 1,2,3)
Added 'The' (ال) to make it a title of Allah, and changed the ending to match with Allah's name. The long 'aa' sound is written with a small line above.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added shadda and alif to show this is someone who does this action intensively and repeatedly
Added ال to show this is referring to Allah's attribute as The Supreme Overpowering One
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to لِلَّهِ (belonging to Allah) in the ayah
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it a proper word
Adding 'ٱلْ' makes it specific - 'the day' instead of just any day
Adding fatha at end shows this word is receiving the action. Here it's the time when there will be no injustice.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding تُ at the start shows someone is receiving the repayment instead of giving it

steps.m: she/it is repaid (subject of كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject form of the word
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun form. This word belongs to كُلُّ (every) in this ayah.
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is any soul/self (not a specific one) and that it belongs to something (كُلُّ - every)
Basic preposition showing how something is done
Added kasra to help with pronunciation
Added ما which means 'what'. Together with كَسَبَتْ after it, this means 'with what they earned'
Connected the root letters with fathas to create the basic past tense verb meaning 'he earned'

steps.m: she earned (referring to نَفْسٍۭ - a soul)
steps.n: Added ت with sukun at the end to show the subject is feminine (نَفْسٍۭ meaning 'soul' is feminine in Arabic). This word appears in the phrase 'each soul will be rewarded for what she earned'
This is a basic negative particle that negates what comes after it. Here it negates ظُلْمَ (injustice/wrongdoing), saying there will be no injustice.
Added fatha (َ) on ل to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: to be unfair or cause harm to others

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changes from describing an action to naming the concept itself

steps.m: unfairness (object of لا in لا ظُلْمَ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because it comes after لا (no/not) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun. This word is being used to tell when something happens.
Added ال to make it specific and َ at the end to show it answers 'when?'. This word tells when each soul gets its reward.
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that introduces important statements
Adding the kasra and shadda makes this word stronger, showing that what comes after it is very important. Here it introduces the word اللَّهَ (Allah).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being described as swift in reckoning)
steps.n: Changed to show Allah is the one being talked about in the sentence (added marks to first and last letters)
Added ي between second and third root letters to intensify the meaning
Added ُ at the end to show this describes Allah in this ayah
Changes from basic root to noun showing the act of calculating
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to Allah. This word is describing Allah being quick at reckoning (judging people's deeds).

steps.m: to warn or alert about danger

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to warn someone directly' instead of just 'to warn'
Changed from 'he warned' to 'you warn!' by making it a command
Added و meaning 'and' to connect this command with what came before
Added هُمْ to show who is being warned (them)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و is made silent with sukun because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: the day (object of أَنذِرْهُمْ - warn them)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb 'warn them' that comes before it in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added ة to make it feminine, meaning 'the approaching one (feminine)'
Added the definite article ال to specify this particular approaching event (referring to the Day of Judgment)
Added kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because this word is connected to what comes next in the verse
This is a time word that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects to the word يَوْمَ (day) to specify 'when' that day is.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah.
Adds و and changes vowels to make the plural form of 'heart' (قَلْب)

steps.m: the hearts (subject of being at the throats)
steps.n: Adding ال shows these are specific hearts mentioned in the ayah - the hearts that reach up to throats in fear

steps.m: the hearts (subject of being at the throats)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows these hearts are the subject - they are the things that reach the throats
This is a basic preposition that shows location or presence. It doesn't come from a root.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word

steps.m: at, near, in the presence of (of ٱلْحَنَاجِرِ)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. In this ayah, it shows the hearts are at/near the throats.
Connected the root letters with basic vowel marks
The singular 'throat' becomes plural 'throats' by adding an alif after ح and changing the middle to نا and adding ِ (kasra) before the last letter
Added definite article ال (written as ٱلْ) to specify these particular throats
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by 'the hearts' (ٱلْقُلُوبُ) in the phrase 'when the hearts reach the throats'
Changes from verb to noun meaning 'one who does the action'

steps.m: those who are holding back (emotions/anger) (describes القلوب/hearts)
steps.n: Added ين to make it plural, describing multiple hearts in a state of emotional suppression
This is a negative particle that negates both 'close friend' (حَمِيمٍ) and 'intercessor' (شَفِيعٍ) in this ayah. It shows that the wrongdoers will have neither of these things.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed to show the person who does the action
Changed to plural form to show many wrongdoers
Added 'al' (ال) meaning 'the' at the start, which caused the ظ letter to get a shadda (double mark)
Added لِ at the beginning to show 'for/to the wrongdoers'
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects حَمِيمٍ (close friend) to the previous phrase, showing the wrongdoers don't have any close friends.

steps.m: to be hot/warm (especially about close relationships)

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who has this quality strongly. In this ayah, it's saying the wrongdoers won't have any close protector.
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of مِنْ (from/of) in the phrase 'they have no close protector'
Starting with the basic negation particle لا
Added conjunction و to connect this negation to the previous statement. This is negating that there will be any شَفِيعٍ (intercessor).
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show this describes someone who does the action
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (any intercessor) and is the object of the word مِنْ (from/of) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice present tense by adding يُ at start and ُ at end. This describes شَفِيعٍ (intercessor), telling us that the intercessor is someone who is obeyed.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb form
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. This word is connected to خَآئِنَةَ ٱلْأَعْيُنِ (the stealing glances) which it knows/perceives.
Present tense verb meaning 'he knows' - no change needed
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changes from a verb (to betray) to a noun meaning 'betrayer'
Adding ة changes it from 'betrayer' to 'an act of betrayal' - like changing from the person who does it to the thing that was done
Added final َ to show this is the object of يَعْلَمُ (what Allah knows)
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukoon) to make the root letters into the word for 'eye'
Changed to broken plural pattern أَفْعُل which is used for some body parts

steps.m: the eyes (object of يَعْلَمُ - he knows)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it's the object of يَعْلَمُ (he knows)
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation. This word is the subject of تُخْفِى (conceals) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This word connects to يَعْلَمُ (He knows) from the previous part.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: you/she hide(s) (what ٱلصُّدُورُ - the chests contain)
steps.n: Added تُ at beginning to show present tense for 'you' or 'she'. Changed vowels to show present tense pattern. This verb is acting on what is in the chests (ٱلصُّدُورُ) mentioned right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: chest, breast, heart (as the source of feelings/thoughts)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic vowel marks to form the singular noun
Changes from single chest (صَدْر) to many chests (صُدُور) by adding the 'u' sound in the middle
Adds the 'u' sound at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Adds 'the' to make it specific chests rather than any chests
This is Allah's personal name, not derived from a root. It's always written with ال and a shadda on the ل
The ُ (damma) shows that Allah is the one doing the action in this sentence - specifically the judging (يَقْضِى) mentioned right after

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَقْضِى)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this part to what came before. The ٱ is just a different way of writing the first ل when it comes after وَ
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense action
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense. The sukun and kasra show this follows a specific pattern for present tense verbs.
Added ى at the end because this is a special type of verb that ends with a weak letter
Adding the basic past tense vowel mark
Added the preposition bi- to show 'with/by'
Added the definite article to specify 'the truth'
Added kasra ending because it follows the preposition bi-. This word shows how Allah judges (يَقْضِى) - He judges with the truth.
Forms the basic relative pronoun by adding ال and ي to show it refers to someone or something specific
Changes from singular to plural by adding ين at the end
Adds و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word is the subject of يَدْعُونَ (they call/invoke) in the ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The ا changes back to و.
Added نَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This word is parallel to يَقْضُونَ (they judge) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under م to show how to pronounce it correctly. This word connects to دُونِهِۦ (besides/other than Him) to show what something is from.
Adding damma (ُ) to make it a complete word
Adding kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it. This is because it follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
Added هِ meaning 'Him' (referring to Allah) with an elongation mark ۦ to show proper pronunciation
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَقْضُونَ (they judge) that follows it, showing that they cannot judge/decide anything
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى due to being a weak letter at the end.
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end is replaced by و because it's a weak letter.
Added vowels (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the word mean 'a thing'
Added the preposition بِ which shows how something is done or used (in this ayah, it shows what they judge with)

steps.m: with anything (object of يَقْضُونَ - they judge)
steps.n: The word gets dots under it (ٍ) to show it's the thing being used to judge. It connects to the word يَقْضُونَ (they judge) to tell us what they judge with

steps.m: indeed/certainly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ)
steps.n: This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show this emphasis. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' - this is a unique word that became the proper name for the One True God

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the L sound, changed first letter to connecting hamza, and added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Basic form of the independent pronoun meaning 'he'
The word 'he' gets dots added above to show it's doing the action of judging/deciding in this sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he heard' to a special form meaning 'one who hears perfectly' - like changing 'hear' to 'hearer' but much stronger
Added 'The' (ال) at the start to make it a divine name of Allah, with special Quranic spelling (ٱل). The س gets doubled (shadda) after ال

steps.m: The All-Hearing (divine attribute of Allah, subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
The pattern with ي shows someone/something that has this quality in a complete or perfect way
Adding ال and ُ makes this a definite attribute of Allah, showing His perfect and complete ability to see and perceive all things
This particle turns the statement into a question, often with a tone of surprise or emphasis
Adds 'and' to connect this question with previous statements
Adds negation for past time. This combination questions why something hasn't happened, often with a tone of criticism. It connects to the next verb يَسِيرُوا۟ (traveled)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed vowels to make it passive (something being done to)
Added يَ at the beginning to change it from a command to show something happening in the present

steps.m: they travel (in فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - on the earth)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people are doing the action. This word connects to 'on the earth' (فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show where they travel.
Basic preposition showing location - connects 'travel' (يَسِيرُوا۟) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show where the traveling happens
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together. The fatha on first letter shows it's a concrete noun, and the sukun helps pronunciation.
The word gets an -i ending because it comes after في (in/on/at) in the ayah

steps.m: the earth/land (after في in the ayah)
steps.n: Makes the noun definite by adding the definite article
This is the basic concept of looking or observing
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed to plural by adding وا at the end

steps.m: so they look/will look (connected to يَسِيرُوا۟ - they travel)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows from traveling mentioned before (they travel... so they look)
This is a question word that asks about the way or manner something happens
Adding these vowel marks helps show this is a question word about manner
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it past tense 'he' form
Changed to common past tense verb 'kāna' by adding alif after first letter

steps.m: to follow or come after something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to عَاقِبَة by adding ا and ة to make it mean 'the end result' instead of 'to follow'
Added ُ to show this is the subject of كان (was). The alif changed to a long alif (ٰ) in this specific writing style.
Starting with the basic relative pronoun that refers to one person or thing
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال to make it specific and definite

steps.m: those who (referring to people in the past)
steps.n: Added final vowel mark and shadda for proper pronunciation. The hamza at the start becomes a wasl hamza (ٱ) because it connects with the previous word. In this ayah, it refers to previous nations mentioned in the story.
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا at the end to make it 'they were' instead of just 'was', showing many people
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects قَبْلِ (before) with هِمْ (them) to mean 'before them'.
Root letters arranged with fatha and sukun to make the common word meaning 'before'
Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection

steps.m: before them (referring to previous nations mentioned in ٱلَّذِينَ كَانُوا۟)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show 'them' referring to the previous nations mentioned
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding another fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern

steps.m: they were (referring to those who came before)
steps.n: Changed كَونَ to كَانُوا by adding وا at the end to show multiple people (they) instead of one person (he). The word talks about past nations.
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'they' for a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it's the subject of كَانُوا (they were).
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Combined the doubled letters with a shadda mark for easier pronunciation

Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative, showing something is stronger than something else. In this ayah, it's comparing the strength of previous nations to the current people being addressed.
This is a preposition showing source, origin, or connection. It's connecting to the pronoun هُمْ (them) in this ayah.

steps.m: from/of them (referring to the previous nations mentioned)
steps.n: Added هم which means 'them' (masculine plural). In this ayah, it refers back to the previous nations that were mentioned (الَّذِينَ كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ - those who came before them).
Changed from a verb meaning 'to be strong' into a noun meaning 'strength' by adding a 'u' sound at start and making the middle letter stronger
Added ة to make it a specific instance of strength

steps.m: strength (as the object being compared in أَشَدَّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةً - stronger than them in strength)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being compared in the phrase 'stronger than them in strength'

steps.m: to leave a trace or mark behind

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun
Changed from singular 'trace' to plural 'traces' by adding alif at start and middle
Added conjunction 'and' at beginning and split the long آ into ءَا for proper pronunciation

steps.m: and traces or marks (as object of كانوا)
steps.n: Added the -an ending (tanween) to show this word is receiving the action, like saying 'they left traces' instead of just 'traces'

steps.m: in/within/inside (connects ٱلْأَرْضِ 'the earth' to يَسِيرُوا۟ 'they travel')
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something happens. Here it connects 'they travel' with 'the earth' to show where the traveling happens.

steps.m: in/within/inside (connects ٱلْأَرْضِ 'the earth' to يَسِيرُوا۟ 'they travel')

Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'day'
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'in/by' and ِ at the end because it's connected to the word الحِسَابِ (the reckoning) forming 'in/by the Day of Reckoning'
Changed the root into a noun that means the process or result of counting/calculating. This word appears in يَوْمِ ٱلْحِسَابِ (Day of Reckoning).
Added -i ending because it belongs to 'day of' (like saying 'day's reckoning')
Adds definite article prefix to make the noun definite

steps.m: he said (subject of رَجُلٌ مُّؤْمِنٌ - a believing man)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past. The و (waw) in the middle changed to ا (alif) because it's a hollow verb. This word tells us what the believing man did.

steps.m: and he said (connecting to previous ayah)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it
The vowels make it into a noun meaning 'man'
Added double damma to show this is the subject doing the action of concealing (يَكْتُمُ) in this verse
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes verb into noun meaning 'one who does the action' by adding مُ prefix and changing vowels
Makes the meaning stronger by doubling the م sound at the start

steps.m: a believing person (describing the man رَجُلٌ mentioned before)
steps.n: Adds two small marks (ٌ) at the end to show this describes 'a' person (not 'the' person) and connects to رَجُلٌ (man) mentioned earlier
Basic preposition showing where someone or something comes from
Added shadda on م to show it connects strongly to the word before it in pronunciation
Added sukun at the end to show where to stop when pronouncing the word
The root letters transform into a noun meaning 'family' or 'dynasty'
Basic vowel marks (fatha and sukun) added to make the root letters readable as 'awl'
These are actually two different words - آل means 'family/household' and is not derived from أول. They just look similar but have different meanings and origins
The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it (Pharaoh)
This is the basic form of the proper noun for the ruler of ancient Egypt
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added fatḥa at the end to show this is the object of the verb يَكْتُمُ (he conceals). This means someone is concealing something from Pharaoh.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, damma on second root letter, and damma at end. This word connects to إِيمٰنَ (faith) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Connected the root letters together
Changed the word into a noun meaning the act of believing
Added fatha to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَكْتُمُ (he conceals)
Added 'his' to show whose faith it is
Added vowels (َ َ َ) to make it 'he killed' in the past tense
Added ت at start and changed vowels to make it 'you' in present tense instead of 'he' in past tense

steps.m: you all kill/slay (masculine plural, present tense)
steps.n: Added masculine plural suffix to indicate plural subject

steps.m: Do you all kill/slay? (questioning the act of killing someone mentioned in رَجُلًا)
steps.n: Added interrogative prefix أ to express shock and disapproval of their action. This question is rhetorical, expressing disapproval of their intention to kill someone just for saying Allah is his Lord.

steps.m: to walk or move on foot

steps.n: The vowels make it into a noun meaning 'man'

steps.m: a man (object of أَتَقْتُلُونَ - will you kill)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word is receiving the action of killing in the question 'Will you kill?'
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects 'a man' with what he says.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the first letter to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'anna'
Added يَ at the beginning to show present/future tense, and changed middle vowels due to hollow verb pattern
Added fatha at end to show subjunctive mood

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: one who cares for (connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to something coming after it
Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' lord. This word is what the believing man calls Allah when he says (يَقُولُ) that Allah is his Lord.
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being used as an object in the sentence

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the u-mark at the end to show it's in the nominative case

Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. قَدْ is a particle that adds emphasis to past tense verbs (like 'has' or 'did' in English) or shows something is likely with present tense verbs (like 'may' or 'might' in English).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about killing a man just for saying Allah is his Lord.
Basic meaning of coming or arriving
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he came' with a long aa sound
Adds second person plural masculine pronoun suffix meaning 'you all'

steps.m: so he came to you all
steps.n: Adds conjunction prefix فَ meaning 'so/then/and'
Root letters with extra 'y' and vowels to make it mean 'very clear/obvious'
The feminine plural ending shows multiple pieces of evidence. This word is the object (what was brought) in the phrase 'he came to you with clear proofs.'
The word gets 'the' in front of it by adding ال
Preposition بِ added, causing following ال to change form to ٱلْ
The word means 'clear proofs/signs' and is connected to the word 'came' (جَاءَ) in the verse, showing what was brought. The بِ at the start means 'with'.
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin. In this ayah, it connects the word رَبِّ (Lord) with كُمْ (your) to mean 'from your Lord'
Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship

steps.m: lord (belonging to or connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this lord belongs to or is connected to something
كُمْ means 'your' when talking to many people, like saying 'y'all' in English
Compound particle: و (and) + إن (if)
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Added يَ at the start to make it mean 'he is' (present tense) instead of 'he was' (past tense), with vowel changes to match
The ون is dropped because this word appears in a conditional statement (after إِن meaning 'if'). This is a common shortening in Arabic.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show this describes someone who does the action
Added an -an ending (tanween) to show this word is describing what someone 'is' (because of يَكُ). The alif got stretched longer.
Combines two particles: فَ (then/so) and عَلَى (upon/on). This combination shows that one thing leads to another.
Changes the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change.

steps.m: then upon him (referring to كَذِبُهُ - his lie)
steps.n: Adds هِ (him) at the end to show who is affected. In this ayah, it refers back to 'his lie' (كَذِبُهُ) mentioned right after.
Changes from verb 'to lie' into the noun 'a lie'
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action (it's the subject)

steps.m: his lie (which is the subject of فَعَلَيْهِ - then upon him)
steps.n: The word 'lie' gets 'his' added to it (like saying 'his book'). This word connects to فَعَلَيْهِ ('then upon him') in the verse.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it starts the condition 'if he is lying'
Added و to connect this condition with the previous statement. This links to the earlier part where they're discussing whether the person is truthful or not.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added يَ at start to make it present tense ('now' instead of 'before'), middle vowels changed to show it's happening now
Adding alif after first root letter makes it describe someone who does the action. This word is describing what someone might be in this verse.
The ًا ending shows this word is being affected by another word (يَكُ) in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (happening now). The middle letter changed from و to ي to make it easier to say.
The vowel is dropped and the word gets shorter because it comes after 'if' in an if-then statement

steps.m: he hits/affects you all (in the condition وَإِن يَكُ صَادِقًا - and if he is truthful)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who is being affected. This word is part of the condition 'if he is truthful'.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'a part'

steps.m: some/part (subject of يُصِبْكُم - will strike you)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. It's the subject of the verb يُصِبْكُم (will strike/afflict you).
This is a relative pronoun used to connect clauses and describe something mentioned before. The ل of the definite article assimilates with the following ذ creating a shadda.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form
Changes from past to present tense. The و is dropped because it's between two vowels (a common rule for weak letters)

steps.m: he promises you all (object of يَعِدُ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being promised to. This word is connected to the previous words about what might happen to 'you all'
Basic form of the word that shows something depends on something else
Adding the shadda and kasra changes this from a conditional word to one that strongly emphasizes what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah definitely does not guide those who exceed limits.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Added ال and merged it with the word, doubling the ل with a shadda. This makes it refer specifically to Allah, the one true God, rather than just any deity.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of يَقُولَ - he says)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being spoken about in the phrase 'he says Allah'. Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَهْدِى (guides) negative, changing its meaning to 'does not guide'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he guided'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Added ى at end to show ongoing action in present tense

This is a relative pronoun that connects to the next statement. Here it introduces the person being described in 'he is a transgressor, a liar.' It's like saying 'The one who...' at the start of a description.

steps.m: he (referring to the person who is مُسْرِفٌ كَذَّابٌ)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers back to someone being described as wasteful and lying in this ayah. It connects what comes before with what comes after.

steps.m: to go beyond proper bounds, to be excessive

steps.n: Adding م at the start and certain vowels turns this into a participle - describing someone who does the action
This word describes the person being talked about in the ayah - it's saying 'he is a transgressor'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda on middle letter makes the action more intense or repeated
Adding alif after the shadda changes it from a verb (action) to a noun describing someone who does this action frequently
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it indefinite, like saying 'a' in English

steps.m: to stand together, establish, rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The root meaning of standing together developed into meaning a group of people who unite as one.
Added يٰ at start to show calling out to someone, and ِ at end to show possession (my people)
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added كم which means 'you all' to show who the preposition is directed to
Changed the ending to damma because this word is connected to ٱلْمُلْكُ (the dominion) in the ayah, showing that the dominion belongs to you all
Changed the word from an action (controlling) to a thing (power/authority)

steps.m: the dominion (subject of لَكُمُ - belongs to you)
