steps.m: from (connects دُونِ to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects دُونِ (besides/instead of) to the previous context.

steps.m: to be lower than or without something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'without' or 'besides'
Adding kasra shows this word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'besides Allah'
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'

This is the unique name for the One God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda make it specific and emphasize there is only one true God. The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes before it.

steps.m: he said (followed by ضَلُّوا۟ - they went astray)
steps.n: Changed from basic root 'to say' into past tense 'he said' by adding fatha marks and changing middle و to ا. Links to next word 'they went astray'

steps.m: they said (followed by ضَلُّوا۟ - they went astray)
steps.n: Adding وا shows that a group (they) did the action instead of just one person (he). This matches with ضَلُّوا۟ (they went astray) which comes right after.

steps.m: to lose the way, stray from the right path

steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he went astray'
When two same letters come together, we combine them with a shadda (ّ)
Added وا to show many people did this action
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from or separating from something else
Added نا (meaning 'us') at the end. The نْ of عَنْ combines with the ن of نا to make a stronger ن sound (shown by ّ)

steps.m: rather/but rather (contradicting what was said before about ضَلُّوا۟)
steps.n: This word is used to correct or contradict what was said before. Here it contradicts their claim that their false gods were 'lost from them' by revealing that these gods never existed in the first place.
This is a negative word that makes verbs show something didn't happen in the past, like saying 'did not' in English
Added emphasis by doubling the ل sound with a shadda mark
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يَ with نَ
The word لَمْ before this word makes it past tense and removes the final ُ

steps.m: to call upon, invite, or pray to

steps.n: The ي at the start shows this is happening now (present tense)
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added emphasis by doubling the ن sound

steps.m: we certainly used to call/invite/pray to
steps.n: Added ا (alif) at the end to show this was something we used to do in the past
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with the word قَبْلُ (before) to show 'from before'.

steps.m: to be in front of or before something

steps.n: Added fatha and sukun to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject in the sentence

steps.m: thing (acted on by نَدْعُوا - we call/invoke)
steps.n: The root letters ش-ي-أ became the noun شَيْء (thing) by adding the vowel marks - a fatha on ش and sukoon on ي. This is the object being called/worshipped.
Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show this is indefinite (any thing) and is being acted upon. This is what they claim they weren't calling upon/worshipping before.
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Adding ٰلِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ at the end makes it point to something further away or previously mentioned

steps.m: like that, similarly (pointing to what was mentioned before about those who were worshipped besides Allah being lost)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning makes it mean 'like that' or 'similarly', referring back to something previously mentioned

steps.m: to lose the way or deviate from the right path

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'causing' someone to lose their way
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start. Both words mean making someone else go astray
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'

This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the shadda on ل make it specific and definite. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence.

steps.m: to reject, to disbelieve, to be ungrateful

steps.n: Changed to active participle form describing someone who does the action
Changed ending to -i sound because it follows a preposition or is possessed by another word
Added ين ending to show many disbelievers being acted upon

steps.m: the disbelievers (being led astray by Allah)
steps.n: Makes the word definite and shows these are specific disbelievers being mentioned. In this ayah, they are the objects of Allah's action of leading astray.
The alif with a line over it (ٰ) changes 'this' to 'that', showing we're pointing to something farther away
Added ل with kasra (لِ) which is part of the demonstrative structure

steps.m: that is for you all (addressing multiple people)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is addressing multiple people, like saying 'that for you all'
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'with', 'by', or 'because of'

steps.m: because of what (connects to تَفْرَحُونَ - you rejoice)
steps.n: Added ما (what) to create a compound meaning. This word connects to تَفْرَحُونَ (you rejoice) to ask about the reason for their rejoicing.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Because و is a weak letter between two vowels, it gets removed and changes the first vowel to match the dropped و
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Changed from 'I was' to 'you all were' by replacing تُ (meaning I) with تم (meaning you all). Shows multiple people being spoken to. Links to تَفْرَحُونَ to tell what they were doing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to make it present tense (happening now), vowel changes show it's ongoing rather than finished

steps.m: you (all) rejoice (because of what you used to do)
steps.n: Added ون to show plural 'you all'. This word explains why the previous actions happened, connected to بِمَا كُنتُمْ (because of what you used to do)
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects to الْأَرْضِ (the earth) to show where something happens.
Added ى at the end to complete the preposition. This shows it's connecting to what comes after it (الْأَرْضِ - the earth).
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters together. The fatha goes on the first letter, and sukun on the middle letter to show it's not moving.
The kasra ending shows this word follows فِى (in/on) in the ayah

steps.m: the earth (being acted upon by فِى)
steps.n: Adding definite article to make the noun definite
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a noun meaning 'other than'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word الْحَقِّ (the truth) that comes after it
Added بِ at start to mean 'with', which combines with 'other than' to mean 'without'
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create the noun meaning 'truth'
Added ال to make it specific/definite
The word means 'truth' and gets a kasra (ee sound) at the end because it comes after words like 'of' or 'without'. Here it follows the word 'without' (بِغَيْرِ)
This is a basic preposition showing cause or reason
Added ما which makes it refer to something general or unknown

steps.m: and because of what (connects to تَفْرَحُونَ - you rejoice)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about rejoicing (تَفْرَحُونَ)
Added vowels to connect the letters together and make the past tense form
The word gets shorter by dropping the middle letter و and changing the first vowel to ُ to make it a command meaning 'Be!'
Adding تُ shows who did the action (you)

steps.m: you all were (referring to those who rejoiced wrongfully)
steps.n: Adding مْ makes it refer to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to be excessively joyful or boastful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at the start, adding sukun on middle letter, and changing last vowel to damma
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start

steps.m: you all are being excessively joyful (because of your wrongful joy on earth)
steps.n: Added plural ending ون to show multiple people are being addressed. The context from the ayah shows this is referring to their wrongful joy on earth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding alif with damma at start, putting sukun on middle letter, and changing vowels
Changed from commanding one person to commanding many people by adding وا at the end. The alif at start became ٱ since it connects to previous words.
Connected the letters and changed middle و to ا to make the basic singular noun form
Changed to plural form. This word is what is being entered in the command 'Enter!' (ادْخُلُوا)
Changed the middle alif to a small alif (ٰ) above the letter - both forms are correct spellings of the same word meaning 'doors'

steps.m: place of intense fire and punishment
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from Hebrew 'Gehinnom', referring to the place of punishment in the afterlife. Starting with the basic consonants.
Added vowels and doubled the ن with shadda to show intensity of the punishment
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (thing being entered) in the phrase 'enter Hell'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: lasting forever (describes the state of entering أَبْوٰبَ/gates)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (he lasted) to a describing word (lasting) - like changing 'run' to 'running'

steps.m: ones lasting forever (describes those entering the gates)
steps.n: Added ين to show this describes multiple people. The alif changed to a long alif (ٰ) in this form.
Basic preposition showing location
Added ها which refers back to Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This connects the preposition to what it's talking about.
Adding vowels changes this into a common word meaning 'how evil/bad'

steps.m: and how evil/bad (is the destination of the arrogant)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this evaluation with the previous statement about entering Hell, showing this is the consequence

steps.m: dwelling place (of الْمُتَكَبِّرِينَ - the arrogant ones)
steps.n: Added مَ at beginning to show place where action happens, and ى at end to make it a noun. This word describes Hell as the permanent residence for the arrogant ones mentioned after it.
Added ت at beginning and doubled middle letter to show doing something to oneself
Changed from a past tense verb (he was arrogant) to a noun meaning someone who is arrogant
Changed from singular (one arrogant person) to plural (many arrogant people) by adding ين at the end
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific arrogant people
Adding imperative command form vowels and prefix
Added helper alif since Arabic words can't start with a sukun
Added فَ at beginning to connect this command with what came before it, showing it's a result or consequence
Added kasra (ِ) under the first letter to show how it's pronounced
Added shadda (ّ) to make the meaning stronger. This word emphasizes that 'Allah's promise is true' in this ayah.
Root letters made into noun by adding vowel marks (fatha and sukoon)
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/certainly) in the phrase 'indeed, Allah's promise'
Added shadda to show that the lam is doubled in Allah's name
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of وَعْدَ (promise). The alif at the start changes to a wasl form since it comes after another word.
Added fatha and doubled the last letter to make it a noun meaning 'truth'
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject. This word describes Allah's promise (وَعْدَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a choice or condition. The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show this is a strong condition.

steps.m: then if/and if (connects to previous command فَٱصْبِرْ)
steps.n: The فَ connects this condition to the previous command 'be patient' (فَٱصْبِرْ), showing what should happen after being patient
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense 'saw' to present tense 'show' by adding نُ prefix
Added نَّ for emphasis, with fatha before it to connect the sounds. This makes the meaning stronger and more certain.

steps.m: we will surely show you (object of نَعِدُ 'we promise')
steps.n: Added كَ to show who receives the action of showing. This word is connected to نَعِدُ (we promise) in the ayah, where both refer to the same 'you'.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun form
The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of the verb نُرِيَنَّكَ (We show you) in the ayah
Base demonstrative form used to point to something or someone
Adding ال with shadda makes it specifically point to something mentioned in the sentence. Here it connects to the verb نَعِدُهُمْ (we promise them).
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense 'we' form. The و from the root drops because it's a weak letter at the start
Added هُمْ at the end to show who receives the promise

This is a conjunction that gives a choice between two possibilities: either showing Muhammad (SAW) some of what was promised, or causing him to pass away. It connects the verbs نُرِيَنَّكَ (We show you) and نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ (We cause you to die).

steps.m: to complete or take in full

steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense and complete
Adding تَ at the start makes this word about receiving/taking something to oneself (in this case, taking souls)
Adding نَ shows 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: we will surely take someone's soul
steps.n: Adding نَّ makes the statement emphatic/certain

steps.m: we will surely take your soul (cause you to die)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows you are the one affected by the action
Starting with the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the next part
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'to us'. This word connects to يُرْجَعُونَ (they are returned) in this ayah, showing they are returned to Us (Allah).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive form by adding يُ at start and changing vowels to show the action is being done to someone
Added ون to show multiple people are being returned
This is the base particle that shows certainty or completion of an action
Adding ل at the beginning adds extra emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Adding و at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding أَ at start makes the sending more forceful or deliberate. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ).
Adding نا at the end changes 'he sent' to 'We sent' - the نا means 'We' and shows We are doing the sending
Added damma vowels to make it a plural noun meaning 'messengers'
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of sending (We sent messengers)
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from somewhere or is part of a group
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م in speech, making the م sound stronger. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make the word mean 'before'
Added the pronoun 'you' (ك) at the end. The ِ before ك shows this word follows مِن (from/of) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing something coming from somewhere or someone. The kasra (ِ) and sukun (ْ) are added to show it's a complete preposition.

steps.m: from them (referring to the messengers mentioned in قَبْلِكَ)
steps.n: Added هم which means 'them' for a group of males. In this ayah, it refers back to the messengers (رُسُلًا) mentioned earlier.
This is a question word that asks about people or introduces relative clauses about people (like 'who' or 'whoever' in English)

The shadda is added because this word comes after another word ending in a similar sound. This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic. In this ayah, it refers to some of the messengers who Allah did not tell their stories to Prophet Muhammad.

steps.m: to tell stories repeatedly or in detail

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and make the basic past tense form

steps.m: we narrated/told in detail (to عَلَيْكَ - to you)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and changed the last fatha to sukun before the suffix. This word connects to عَلَيْكَ (to you) later in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of or upon something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun

steps.m: upon you (object of نَقْصُصْ - we narrate)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this affects 'you' (one male). This word shows who is being narrated to in the verb نَقْصُصْ (we narrate).
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added the plural masculine pronoun 'them' to show who is being referred to. This refers back to the messengers (رُسُلًا) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added the conjunction 'and' at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous mention of messengers
This is a question word that asks about people. Here it's used to mean 'whoever' because it's referring to some of the messengers (رُسُلًا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
The shadda (doubling mark) is added because this word comes after مِنْهُم which ends in a vowel. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a basic negative word that makes verbs show something did not happen in the past. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Added emphasis to the first letter. This is common when certain words come before لَمْ.

steps.m: to tell a story or narrate events in sequence

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make it a past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he told' to a command 'tell!' by removing middle vowel and changing vowels
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else

steps.m: upon you (connected to قَصَصْنَا - we narrated)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this affects 'you' (when speaking to one male). This word connects to قَصَصْنَا (we narrated) to show what was narrated upon/to.
Starting with the basic word 'what' which is used to ask questions or make negations

steps.m: and what (connects to لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا - indeed We sent)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about sending messengers
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he was' in the past tense
The middle letter و changed to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added لِ at the beginning to mean 'for' and tanween at the end to show it's indefinite (any messenger). This word is the object of كَانَ (it was) in this ayah.

This is a connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the idea that 'it was not for a messenger' with what they could/couldn't do - 'to bring a sign'. It works like 'that' in English phrases like 'I know that he came'.
Present tense verb made by adding يَ at start. The middle letter has a sukun mark. Shows 'he' is doing the action.
Adds fatha at end to show this is after أَن (that)
Root letters were turned into a noun by adding special vowels to make it mean 'one sign/miracle'
Added بِ at start to mean 'with/by' and tanween kasra at end to show it's indefinite. This word is what comes/is brought in the phrase 'to come with a sign'

steps.m: except (connects to يَأْتِىَ - meaning no messenger could come with a sign except...)
steps.n: This is an exception particle that shows something is excluded from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that bringing signs only happens with Allah's permission.
Changed the action of allowing into the noun form meaning 'permission'
Added بِ at beginning to show this is done through or by means of permission. This connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) which comes right after it in the ayah.
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because it comes after بِ (bi)
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The first ا changes to ل and gets a shadda to show emphasis.
Added ِ at the end to show this describes whose command it is. The ا at the start changes to ٱ for easier pronunciation when connecting words.

steps.m: when (introduces a condition that leads to جَآءَ 'came')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when Allah's command comes.

steps.m: then when (connects to previous ayah and introduces جَآءَ 'came')
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition to what was mentioned before. It shows this is what happens next after the previous verse talked about Allah's permission.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming
Changed from action to noun meaning the command itself. This word is possessed by اللَّهِ (Allah) in this ayah.
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on ل, and changed initial alif. This shows Allah is the owner of the permission mentioned in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: it was decided/settled (by أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ - the command of Allah)
steps.n: Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it. This word shows what happened when Allah's command came.
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to make it stronger. This turns the action into a noun.
Adding ال makes it specific - not just any truth, but THE truth

steps.m: with/by the truth (describes how قُضِىَ/it was judged was done)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows how the judgment was made - with truth and justice. This word explains how the judgment mentioned in قُضِىَ was carried out.
Adding vowels makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'to lose'
Added وَ at the beginning to mean 'and'
This is the basic word meaning 'here' that points to a nearby place
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'here' (close) to 'there' (far away)
Adding كَ makes it point specifically to that situation or place being discussed. In this ayah, it refers to the moment when Allah's command comes.

steps.m: one who makes things false/invalid (doer of خَسِرَ)
steps.n: Active participle form showing someone who does the action. This word is connected to خَسِرَ (lost) in the ayah, showing who lost.

steps.m: the one who makes things false/invalid
steps.n: Added the definite article ال to make it specific
Added ون to make it plural masculine. These falsifiers are the ones who lost according to خَسِرَ in the ayah.

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the concept of worship into a word for the one who is worshipped

Added ال to show this is THE one true deity, doubled the ل to emphasize its special nature, and added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence. The أ changed to ل and the ه lost its vowel mark because this is a special word used only for Allah.
This is the basic relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things. Here it connects to the verb جَعَلَ (made/placed) to tell us who did the action.

steps.m: The One who (connects to جَعَلَ - made/placed)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning which turns into ٱلّ (with shadda) because the ل merges with the ذ. This makes it specific, pointing to Allah in this case.

steps.m: to make, set, or place something
he made (for لَكُمُ - for you all)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past. This verb is connected to لَكُمُ which shows he made something specifically for you all.
Added fatha to show this is connected to what follows

steps.m: for you all (recipients of جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: Added the plural 'you' pronoun كُمُ to show who the action is for. This word connects to جَعَلَ (made) to show Allah made something for you all.
Basic noun form referring to cattle/livestock as they were seen as a blessing and source of comfort
Changes from single animal (نَعَم) to plural form (أَنْعَام) to show many different kinds of animals
Makes the noun definite by adding ال
Shows this word is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adds تَ at start to mean 'you' and changes vowels to make it present tense
Adds وا at the end to make it 'you all' instead of 'you'

steps.m: so that you (all) may ride (purpose of جَعَلَ/made)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose - this explains why Allah made these animals for us
Basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from it/her (referring to الْأَنْعٰمَ - the cattle)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end to show this refers back to the cattle (الْأَنْعٰمَ) mentioned earlier in the ayah. The feminine form is used because groups of animals are typically treated as feminine in Arabic.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous one, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: and from it/her (referring to الْأَنْعٰمَ - the cattle)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show we're talking about the cattle (which is feminine in Arabic). This connects back to الْأَنْعٰمَ (the cattle) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels - a common pattern for many verbs
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Added و at the beginning to connect this idea with the previous one
Added كم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
Basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added ها at the end to show this means 'in it' where 'it' refers to something feminine. In this ayah, it refers to the animals mentioned earlier that Allah has made for us.

steps.m: to provide benefit or be useful

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show where or what provides benefit
Added ا after first letter to make plural form showing multiple benefits
Added ُ at the end to show this word is the subject. The alif is written as ٰ but pronounced the same.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at a destination

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense (balaga) to present tense (yablugu) by changing the vowels
Added تَ (ta) at start to make it 'you' in present tense
Added لِ prefix to indicate purpose/intent
Added plural masculine subject pronoun suffix وا to indicate plural 'you'

steps.m: and in order that you (plural) reach
steps.n: Added conjunction 'and'
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to the benefits mentioned)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what/who is being referred to. Here it refers back to the benefits (منافع) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is the basic concept of needing something
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding vowels and turning و into ا
Added ة to make it a noun referring to one specific instance of needing
Added tanween (ً) to show this word is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to the word صُدُورِكُمْ (your chests/hearts) to show where something is located.
The ى at the end shows this word is connecting to what comes after it. It's like a bridge between words that shows where something is located.

steps.m: chest, breast, heart (as the source from which things come)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic vowels to form the singular noun
Changes the singular noun to a broken plural form to show multiple chests/hearts
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something
Added كُمْ at the end to show these are your (plural) chests/hearts
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on top of'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed the ending to prepare for adding a pronoun
Added ها at the end to show this means 'upon it' (referring back to something feminine mentioned before)
Combines conjunction و (and) with preposition على (upon)
Added damma and sukun to create the noun form meaning 'ship' - possibly named this way because ships follow curved paths through the water
Added the definite article ال (written as ٱلْ) at the beginning to make it specific. The كِ at the end shows this is the object of the word عَلَى (upon) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he carried' to 'is being carried' by adding تُ at start and changing middle vowel to show something is receiving the action
Added ون to show this refers to multiple people being carried
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense and causative form (making someone else see)
Added suffix to show who is being shown (you all)

steps.m: and he shows you all (His signs)
steps.n: Added conjunction 'and' at beginning. This word connects to ءَايٰتِهِۦ (His signs) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be evident

steps.n: Root letters were shaped into a noun pattern with long 'aa' sound to mean 'one sign/proof'
Changed from singular to plural form
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added pronoun هِ (his) and changed spelling of initial hamza
Adding the basic vowel mark to help pronounce the word
Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from what was mentioned before. This word is asking about which of Allah's signs (ءَايٰتِ ٱللَّهِ) are being denied.

steps.m: to show, appear, or become clear

steps.n: Root letters change to make the noun 'sign' by adding long aa sound at start
Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end
Changes to show it belongs to Allah: combines two alifs into one long alif and adds 'i' sound at end
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity'

This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite and the shadda emphasizes the L sound. The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence.

steps.m: to deny or refuse to accept something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you all deny (referring to multiple people being addressed)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before it

steps.m: And did they not (before يَسِيرُوا۟ - travel)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to turn this into a question. This question is connected to the next word يَسِيرُوا۟ (travel/journey).

steps.m: to move from place to place
steps.n: The middle letter ي is a weak letter that affects how the word is formed
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show present/future tense, and changed the ending vowel to ُ
Added وا at the end to show that many people are doing the action
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it connects 'they travel' (يَسِيرُوا) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show where the traveling happens.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added ال to make it specific (the earth) and ِ at the end because it comes after the word في (in)

steps.m: to look at something carefully to understand it

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to ُ
Changed from singular 'he' to plural 'they' by adding وا at the end

steps.m: so they look carefully (connected to يَسِيرُوا۟ - they travel)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows from traveling mentioned before (they travel... so they look)
This is a question word that doesn't come from a typical root
Adding vowel marks to make the question word 'how'
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it past tense 'he was'
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and ة at the end to make it mean 'the thing that comes after' (the outcome)
Added damma at the end because this word is the subject of كَانَ (was). Changed alif to alif with horizontal line above for Quranic writing style.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make it relative
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to someone or something
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to multiple people
Adding the proper pronunciation marks and connecting it to the verb كَانُوا (they were) that comes after it in the ayah

This is a preposition that connects to قَبْلِهِمْ (before them) to show where something starts from in time. In this ayah, it's used to talk about people who came before the current people being addressed.
Root letters got vowels to make a noun meaning 'before' in time
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added هِمْ to show this means 'them'. This word comes after مِن (from) in the ayah to say 'from before them'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common for hollow verbs
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added optional pronunciation marks (the small alif ٓ and the small circle ۟) that don't change the meaning
Added vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'it increased/became many'
Added أ at the start to change 'became many' into 'more numerous' - like adding '-er' in English to make something comparative. Links to مِنْهُمْ (than them) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'
Adding attached pronoun هم to indicate 'them' (masculine plural)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Combined the repeated letters with a shadda mark
Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative (like adding -er in English)

steps.m: and more intense/stronger (than them in strength)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this word with the previous comparison. This word is comparing the strength of past nations to the current people.
The middle letter is doubled to show intensity
Adding ة makes it into a specific instance of strength
The ً (tanween) at the end shows this word is indefinite and is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: to leave a mark, trace, or sign behind

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'mark/trace' by adding vowel marks
Changed to plural form showing multiple traces or signs
Added the tanween to show this word is receiving the action (object) in relation to كانوا (they were)
Added و to connect this word to what came before it, and split the آ into وَءَا to show proper pronunciation
This is a basic preposition showing location or place. In this ayah, it shows where people should travel - in/through the earth.
Added kasra (ِ) mark under ف to show how to pronounce it as 'fi'
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation - it means the same thing
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/on) in the ayah
Basic question/negation word that asks about things or makes them negative
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Added the long alif (آ) to show proper pronunciation length
Added vowels to make past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show causing something to happen. The ي changes to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: it availed (عَنْهُم what they used to earn)
steps.n: Added alif khanjariya (ٰ) to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it's talking about how their wealth and power didn't help them or benefit them in the end.
This is a preposition that shows something moving away or separating from something else. In this ayah, it's used with أَغْنَىٰ (availed) to show what didn't help them.

steps.m: away from them (referring to what they earned)
steps.n: Adding هُم shows this applies to 'them' (a group). Here it refers back to what the previous nations earned or acquired.
This is a basic question word that asks about things (not people). It can also connect parts of sentences together.

steps.m: what (object of يَكْسِبُونَ - they used to earn)
steps.n: Added a shadda on the م to show emphasis and connect it smoothly with the previous word. In this ayah, this word is asking about the things that they used to earn.
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha (َ) completes the basic past tense verb pattern
Changed from 'he was' to 'they were' by adding وا at the end. Connected to أَكْثَرَ to tell us who was 'more numerous'.
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha marks
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and adjusting vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a time particle that shows something happened after another thing

steps.m: Then when (followed by جَآءَتْهُمْ - came to them)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something before it
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks
Added ت with sukun to show this is about something feminine arriving
Added هُمْ with damma and sukun to show the signs came specifically to them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned earlier)
The و makes it mean someone who does this action as their main role
Changed from one messenger (رَسُول) to many messengers (رُسُل) by changing the middle vowel and dropping the long vowel
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in جَآءَتْ (they came)
Added هم at the end to show these are the messengers belonging to them
This root carries the basic meaning of clarity or being evident
The doubled letter makes the meaning more intense, transforming from 'clear' to 'very clear/evident'
Adding ات makes it plural for non-human things

steps.m: with the clear proofs (that the messengers came with)
steps.n: Added both the preposition بِ (with/by) and the definite article ال (the). The kasra at the end shows this word follows the preposition بِ.

steps.m: he became joyful (in response to رُسُلُهُم their messengers)
steps.n: Shows emotional reaction to the messengers mentioned in the ayah. The fatha-kasra-fatha pattern is common for emotional states.

steps.m: they became joyful (referring to those who rejected رُسُلُهُم their messengers)
steps.n: The plural form shows this describes a group's reaction to their messengers. The next ayah shows their joy was misplaced.

steps.m: they became joyful (with emphasis on the group)
steps.n: The small mark above shows this is definitely plural 'they', not 'he'
Basic word meaning 'what' - this is a particle without a root

steps.m: with what (that they had of عِندَهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ - knowledge they possessed)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this is about doing something with or by means of something. In this ayah, it refers to what knowledge they had.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make the word readable

steps.m: with them (referring to رُسُلُهُم - their messengers)
steps.n: Added هُم to show this refers to 'them' (the messengers mentioned earlier in the verse)
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added shadda to emphasize the م sound. This happens because the word before it ends with a similar sound that merges with this م.
Added fatha at the end to connect this word to the next word العِلْمِ (knowledge). Together they mean 'from knowledge'.
Changed the verb (to know) into a noun (knowledge) by adding the special vowel pattern. This pattern is used for making abstract concepts into real things.
Added ٱلْ at beginning to mean 'the' and ِ at end because this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah. The word refers back to the knowledge these people had.
Connected the letters and changed the middle letter ي to ا because it's a hollow verb. This word is connected to what they were mocking (يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ) in the ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it in the ayah
Added kasra to show how the letter is pronounced
Added هِ to show who or what is being referred to
Changed from singular (him) to plural (them) by adding م
This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb فَرِحُوا (they rejoiced), referring to what they had of knowledge
The shadda shows this word connects smoothly to the word before it when reading
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Added وا to show multiple people (they). This word is connected to يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (they mock) in the ayah, showing what the same group was doing.
Basic preposition showing connection or means

steps.m: with him/it (referring to their knowledge/science they had)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ to show what/who is being referred to

steps.m: with him/it (referring to their knowledge/science they had)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide

steps.m: to mock or make fun of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added 'است' at the start to show someone is really making fun of others in a mean way
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic particle meaning 'when' that shows something happened after another thing happened. It's not derived from a root.

steps.m: Then when (followed by رَأَوْا۟ - they saw)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens right after something else. In this ayah, it introduces what happened when they saw something.
Changed from basic root 'to see' into 'he saw' by adding fathas. The alif at the end (ى) shows it's past tense. This happened before they spoke.
Added وْا to show many people did this action. The small mark after the word (۟) helps show correct pronunciation.
Changed the action into a noun. This word is what they saw in the phrase 'they saw Our might'.

steps.m: our might (object of رَأَوْا - they saw)
steps.n: Added نَا to show this is Allah's might that they saw.
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
The word changed from 'he said' to 'they said' by adding وا at the end and changing the middle letter from وَ to ا

steps.m: they said (subject of ءَامَنَّا - they believed)
steps.n: Added pronunciation marks that don't change the meaning. This word is connected to ءَامَنَّا (they believed) which comes after it in the verse.
Adding alif makes it mean 'to actively put trust in something'
Added shadda to emphasize the action
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word is what was said after قَالُوٓا۟ (they said) in the ayah.
This is the base word الله, which is the proper name of God in Arabic. It doesn't come from a typical root pattern.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added a small diagonal line (kasra) under the last letter to show this is connected to the word before it
Changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word
Added بِ at the beginning to show the relationship 'in' or 'with'
Forms a noun meaning the state of being alone
Adds fatha to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Adds pronoun suffix to specify who is alone. In this ayah, it refers to believing in Allah alone without partners.

steps.m: to reject or disbelieve in something

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this action to their previous statement of belief

Changed from 'he' to 'we' by adding نَا at the end. The ْ (sukun) is added before نَا according to Arabic rules. This is part of what the people said, connected to قَالُوٓا۟ (they said) earlier in the ayah.
Basic form of the relative pronoun that means 'what' or 'that which'
Added the preposition بِ to show this describes how they were associating partners with Allah. This بِ connects to مُشْرِكِينَ (those who associate partners) later in the ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'was' to command form 'be!' by changing vowels
Added نَّا to show 'we' did this action in the past. This word is part of what was said (قَالُوٓا۟) when they saw Allah's might.
Basic preposition showing connection or means
Added pronoun هِ to show who/what is being referred to
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Active participle form showing someone who does the action

steps.m: those who make partners (with Allah) (object of كُنَّا - we were)
steps.n: Makes the word plural and shows it's being acted upon by the verb كُنَّا (we were) in this ayah
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to their opposite in the past tense

steps.m: and then not (in past) (connects to يَكُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from previous events. This word connects to يَكُ (was) in the ayah.
The و is a weak letter that affects how the word is formed
Basic past tense form - added vowel marks to make 'he was'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Removed final نُ to create shortened version
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: he benefits them (subject of لَمْ يَكُ)
steps.n: The word 'them' (هُمْ) was added to show who is being benefited. This word tells us what was not happening in the ayah because it comes after لَمْ يَكُ (was not)
This is the basic past tense form of the verb
Basic root letters أ-م-ن get short vowels (َ) to make the past tense verb meaning 'felt safe'

steps.m: to cause to feel safe/to believe
steps.n: Adding آ at start changes meaning from 'feel safe' to 'make safe/believe'
Adding damma at end shows this is in nominative case
Changed from verb 'believe' to noun 'faith' and adds 'their' at the end. This word receives the action of 'benefit' in this ayah.
This is an emphasis particle that starts the word
Added مَّا (when) to لَ (indeed) to mean 'indeed when'
Added ا at the end to complete the particle. This word connects to رَأَوْا۟ (they saw) in the ayah, showing when something happened.
These are the basic letters that give us the meaning of seeing or perceiving something
Added fatha marks and the weak letter ى to make the past tense singular form

steps.m: they saw (when they saw بَأْسَنَا our punishment)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did the action. The ى changes to و because it comes before وا. This word connects to بَأْسَنَا (our punishment) which is what they saw.
Added vowel marks to connect letters and make it a noun meaning the quality itself

steps.m: our might/punishment (object of رَأَوْا - they saw)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us. This word is what they saw (object of رَأَوْا)

steps.m: to establish a custom or way of doing things

steps.n: The damma and sukun marks show this is about established customs/ways
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of the custom
The ة at the end changed to ت because it's connected to another word (Allah) - this is a common pattern when joining words in Arabic
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, changing it from 'a god' to 'The God' (Allah)
Added kasra at the end to show possession, and changed alif to wasl-alif. This word is possessed by سُنَّتَ (practice/way) in this ayah.
This is the base feminine relative pronoun used to connect clauses, like 'that' or 'which' in English when referring to feminine things
Added ال which becomes ٱلّ due to the sun letter ت. This word refers back to سُنَّتَ (way/practice) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This particle is used to emphasize that something has definitely happened in the past. Here it emphasizes that Allah's way has indeed already passed among His servants.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

steps.m: she passed by (describing سُنَّتَ ٱللَّهِ - Allah's way)
steps.n: Changed to feminine past tense by replacing و with ت. This word describes سُنَّتَ ٱللَّهِ (Allah's way) which is feminine in Arabic.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects to the word عِبَادِهِۦ (His servants) to show where something happens.
This form turns the verb into a noun meaning 'one who serves'
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'servant'
Changes singular 'servant' to plural form meaning 'servants' or 'worshippers'
Adds kasra to show possession relationship
Adds pronoun suffix to show possession by Allah
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: and he lost (connected to رَأَوْا۟ - they saw)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous action of 'they saw'. This shows the loss happened after they saw.
This is the basic word used to point to a nearby place
Adding لِ makes the location more distant

steps.m: there/at that place or time (in this context: at that moment when they saw Our punishment)
steps.n: Adding كَ makes it point specifically to something mentioned in the context - here it refers to the moment when the disbelievers saw Allah's punishment
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: one who rejects/denies (subject of خَسِرَ - lost)
steps.n: Changes from 'he rejected' (verb) to 'one who rejects' (doer). Like changing 'deny' to 'denier'. This person is doing the losing (خَسِرَ) in the verse.
Makes the word plural masculine
Adds 'the' (ال) to make it specific which rejecters we're talking about
